text_blocks.text: The National Art Library (also
called Word and Image Department) at
the Victoria and Albert Museum collection
catalog used to be kept in different formats
including printed exhibit catalogs, and card
catalogs. A computer system called MODES
cataloging system was used from the
1980s to the 1990s, but those electronic
files were not available to the library users.
All of the archival material at the
National Art Library is using Encoded
Archival Description (EAD). The Victoria
and Albert Museum has a computer
system but most of the items in the
collection, unless those were newly
accessioned into the collection, probably do
not show up in the computer system. There
is a feature on the Victoria and Albert
Museum web-site called "Search the
Collections," but not everything is listed
there.
